item.title: Seoul Apartment Price Gains Pick Up Pace, Adding Pressure on BOK
item.description: A housing rally in South Korea’s capital gained momentum, reinforcing the case for the nation’s central bank to shift to a hawkish tilt later this month as it grapples with mounting inflationary pressures from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Polish Economic Risks Top Inflation Concerns for MPC’s Wnorowski
item.description: Poland’s economic growth may face a bigger jolt from the Middle East conflict than the country’s inflation, according to Monetary Policy Council member Henryk Wnorowski.

item.title: BOJ’s Masu Says Rate Hike Desirable at ‘Earliest Stage Possible’
item.description: A Bank of Japan board member called for interest rates to be increased as soon as possible provided there is no indication of the economy running into trouble, citing more enduring inflationary risks from the war in Iran.

item.title: Why the ECB’s June Interest-Rate Hike Is Becoming Less Certain
item.description: Two weeks after Christine Lagarde put investors on alert that the European Central Bank is moving toward raising interest rates, a hike in June is becoming less obvious.

item.title: Japan’s Eneos Buys Chevron Asia Oil Assets for $2.2 Billion
item.description: Japanese refiner Eneos Holdings Inc. agreed to purchase some of Chevron Corp.’s refining and retail assets across the Asia-Pacific for $2.17 billion, significantly expanding its footprint across the region.

item.title: Europe Plays Catch-Up in Africa as Emerging Powers Boost Investment
item.description: A summit in Nairobi yielded €14 billion in French investment commitments, but Europeans have fallen far behind newer arrivals in the region.

item.title: Is Polaroid Coming for Today's Stock Market?
item.description: Market concentration in the AI Boom is even worse than in the Nifty Fifty era.

item.title: Global Funds Speed Up Korea Stock Exit as Record Rally Extends
item.description: Foreign investors have deepened their retreat from South Korean stocks, even as the market has kept climbing to new highs on local inflows and the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Japan’s 30-Year Bond Sale Draws Firmer Demand on Higher Yields
item.description: Japan’s 30-year government bond auction Thursday saw slightly stronger demand than the 12-month average as higher yields underpinned buying.

item.title: Copper Retreats From Record Close as Purchases in China Slow
item.description: Copper retreated from a record-high close as the rally started to deter purchases in China, where investors were monitoring the summit between Chinese leader Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump.

item.title: What India’s Top Three Mutual Funds Bought and Sold in April
item.description: Indian investors plowed money into equity funds for a 62nd straight month in April, though inflows eased from the previous month.

item.title: Iran War Gives Xi The Chance to Rekindle Gas Sales With Trump
item.description: As the leaders of the world’s two largest economies meet in Beijing, energy markets will be watching closely for any breakthrough in a dispute that has reshaped global fuel flows: China’s tariffs on US oil and gas.

item.title: Google Tie-Up Lifts Fanuc to Record as Physical AI Bets Grow
item.description: Fanuc Corp. shares soar after the company announced that it would tie up with Alphabet Inc.’s Google in an announcement on Wednesday, broadening expectations for physical AI with robotics companies.

item.title: Metal Stocks Stand Out in India’s Struggling Market
item.description: Nifty Metal Index has overtaken the FMCG gauge in market value.

item.title: Qantas Boss Vanessa Hudson Turns Screw on Ailing Air New Zealand
item.description: Qantas Airways Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Vanessa Hudson is ramping up pressure on struggling Air New Zealand Ltd., adding close to 1 million seats on trans-Tasman routes to squeeze the ailing rival.

item.title: Japanese Crude Tanker Emerges Outside Hormuz in Rare Transit
item.description: A Japanese supertanker has emerged in the Gulf of Oman after last signaling that it was inside the Persian Gulf, indicating a rare, undercover transit through the Strait of Hormuz by a vessel from the Asian country.

item.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Seeks $2 Billion Loan for Malaysia Growth
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-owned data center firm AirTrunk is marketing a $2.3 billion loan to fund a Malaysia project, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a slew of tech-linked financings in Southeast Asia spurred by the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Bagmane REIT Set for Debut in Test for India’s IPO Market
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed Bagmane Prime Office REIT is set to start trading in Mumbai on Thursday after raising $360 million in an initial public offering, a debut that will offer cues on investor appetite and the outlook for India’s equity capital markets.

item.title: Hunt Oil Boss Sees Middle East Conflict as ‘Nightmare Scenario’
item.description: Hunter Hunt, the grandson of famed Texas oil baron H.L. Hunt, is concerned about the impact from damaged Middle Eastern energy infrastructure that could leave oil production lower for years to come.

item.title: World’s Biggest Stock Rally Ignites Speculative Mania in Korea
item.description: In South Korea’s $4.6 trillion stock market, signs of euphoria are popping up everywhere.

item.title: War Turmoil Saps Asian Reserves With Philippines, India Hit Most
item.description: Foreign-exchange reserves are slumping across Asia as policymakers shell out funds to defend their currencies against the spike in oil prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Chip IPO Highlights Malaysia Deals Surging Toward 13-Year High
item.description: Initial public offerings are booming in Malaysia and a wave of upcoming listings including by a chip design firm is powering the market toward the biggest volume in 13 years.

item.title: India Bans Sugar Exports Until End-September to Protect Supply
item.description: India, the world’s second-largest producer of sugar, has banned exports until the end of September, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies.

item.title: Citadel Tells Some Quant Staff to Relocate From HK or Quit, FT Reports
item.description: Citadel has moved some of its researchers out of Hong Kong due to data security concerns, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Blackstone REIT Raises $1.75 Billion in IPO to Buy Data Centers
item.description: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. raised $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, as investors’ appetite for artificial intelligence infrastructure shows no sign of easing.

item.title: UK House Price Gauge Falls to Lowest Since 2023, RICS Says
item.description: More UK estate agents reported falling house prices in April as the war in Iran and elevated borrowing costs hit buyer confidence, according to a closely watched survey.

item.title: China’s Hot, Unprofitable AI Stocks Are Hard to Short Until July
item.description: Short sellers eying China’s hottest AI firms have found the going hard with so little of their shares publicly traded. That scarcity premium may come to an end in July as stock lockups expire.

item.title: AI Bond Binge Overwhelms Wall Street, Pushing Alphabet Overseas
item.description: Bankers were still putting the final touches on Alphabet Inc.’s blockbuster $17 billion of bond sales when word started to spread Monday morning on Wall Street: the company is already hawking more debt.

item.title: ASX Names Former Euronext Paris Chief Anthony Attia as CEO
item.description: ASX Ltd. named Anthony Attia its new chief executive, concluding a search after its former boss Helen Lofthouse said she was standing down earlier this year.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Rising US Inflation Raises Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold held a decline as a resurgence in US inflation reinforced bets the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer.
